props.title: Let’s catch up at this year’s State of the Browser
props.description: We’ll be going along to State of the Browser in London in September 2024.

props.title: Let’s catch up at this year’s State of the Browser
props.author: Paul Love
props.content:
We’re happy to share that we’ll be going along to this year’s [State of the Browser](https://2024.stateofthebrowser.com/)!

## Essential details

* Saturday, 14 September, 2024
* 9:30 am–5:00 pm
* Barbican Centre, London ([Map](https://www.google.co.uk/maps/place/Barbican+Centre/@51.5209863,-0.0957614,17z/data=!4m6!3m5!1s0x48761b56fb64b275:0xc756e26675d21f40!8m2!3d51.5202077!4d-0.0937864!16zL20vMG02cTY?entry=ttu))
* [Ticket information](https://2024.stateofthebrowser.com/tickets/)


## About the event

In case you’ve never been, State of the Browser is an excellent one-day event organised by the lovely and talented people at [London Web Standards](https://londonwebstandards.org/). 

There are a variety of talks about things like web standards, accessibility, and the state of the modern web in general. 

The talks are never not insightful, and it’s a great chance to catch up with web industry people. We highly recommend—hence this post.


## Talk to us

The reason we’re sharing this is that we’ll be going and would love to catch up with you.

Possible topics of conversation:

- Weather
- What you’ve been working on
- Your UI and branding headaches
- Weather
- Design and UI systems generally
- Accessibility
- Coffee?
- Weather

But other than that, we’re pretty relaxed about conversational subject matter.

We don’t plan to speak at the event, but a big thanks to State of the Browser for letting us sponsor.

[Grab a ticket](https://2024.stateofthebrowser.com/tickets/), perhaps?
